title: senna cardiosperma subsp. stowardii
intro: Senna cardiosperma subsp. stowardii, also known as the Stoward's senna or senna cardiosperma, is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the Fabaceae family. Traditionally, Senna cardiosperma subsp. stowardii has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its purgative and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides, such as aloe-emodin and chrysophanol. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat constipation, skin conditions, and inflammatory diseases due to its laxative and anti-inflammatory properties. Senna cardiosperma subsp. stowardii is often prepared as a tea or infusion by infusing the dried leaves and pods in hot water, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional Australian medicine.
